practices.title: Never use eval() or exec() with untrusted input

cwe.name: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')

practices.explanation: eval() and exec() execute arbitrary code. Use json.loads(), ast.literal_eval(), or structured parsers instead.

standards.title: Dynamic code evaluation (eval) MUST NOT be used with user-controlled input
Dynamic code evaluation MUST NOT process user-controlled input

Verify that the application avoids the use of eval() or other dynamic code execution features. Where there is no alternative, any user input being included must be sanitized or sandboxed before being executed.
Verify that when parsing JSON in browsers or JavaScript-based backends, JSON.parse is used to parse the JSON document. Do not use eval() to parse JSON.

practices.title: Never hardcode secrets in source code
practices.explanation: Hardcoded secrets leak through version control. Use environment variables or a secrets manager.

standards.title: Credentials and secrets MUST NOT be hardcoded in source files
Credentials MUST be loaded from secure external sources, never hardcoded

practices.title: Avoid os.system and shell=True — use subprocess with lists

cwe.name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

practices.explanation: os.system and shell=True enable command injection. Use subprocess with argument lists.

standards.title: Shell commands MUST NOT be constructed from user-controlled data
OS commands MUST NOT be constructed from user-controlled data

Verify that the application protects against OS command injection and that operating system calls use parameterized OS queries or use contextual command line output encoding. ([C4](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))
Verify that untrusted file metadata is not used directly with system API or libraries, to protect against OS command injection.

practices.title: Use parameterized queries — never f-string SQL

cwe.name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

practices.explanation: F-string SQL enables injection. Use parameterized queries with the DB-API 2.0 placeholder style.

standards.title: SQL queries MUST use parameterised statements; string concatenation is forbidden
SQL queries MUST use parameterised statements

Verify that data selection or database queries (e.g. SQL, HQL, ORM, NoSQL) use parameterized queries, ORMs, entity frameworks, or are otherwise protected from database injection attacks. ([C3](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

Verify that where parameterized or safer mechanisms are not present, context-specific output encoding is used to protect against injection attacks, such as the use of SQL escaping to protect against SQL injection. ([C3, C4](https://owasp.org/www-project-proactive-controls/#div-numbering))

practices.title: Keep source files under 300 lines

cwe.name: Source Code File with Excessive Number of Lines of Code

practices.bad: # 500-line module mixing models, views, and utilities
practices.good: # Split into models.py, views.py, utils.py — each under 300 lines
practices.explanation: Large files are hard to navigate, review, and test. Extract cohesive groups of functions into separate modules.

standards.title: Source files MUST NOT exceed 300 lines
Functions MUST NOT exceed 50 lines
Source files MUST NOT exceed language-appropriate line limits

practices.title: Keep cyclomatic complexity ≤10 per function
practices.explanation: High cyclomatic complexity makes functions hard to test and understand. Extract conditions into named helper functions.

standards.title: Cyclomatic complexity MUST be ≤10 per function
Functions MUST have ≤5 parameters; use an options object for more
Function cyclomatic complexity MUST be ≤10

practices.title: Never use wildcard imports (from x import *)
practices.explanation: Star imports pollute the namespace, create hidden coupling, and make it impossible to trace where names come from.

standards.title: Wildcard imports (import *) MUST NOT be used
Internal implementation details MUST be hidden behind public interfaces

practices.title: Use type hints on public function signatures
practices.bad:
def calculate_total(items, tax_rate):
    return sum(i.price for i in items) * (1 + tax_rate)
practices.good:
def calculate_total(items: list[Item], tax_rate: float) -> float:
    return sum(i.price for i in items) * (1 + tax_rate)
practices.explanation: Type hints enable static analysis, improve IDE support, and serve as documentation for function contracts.

standards.title: Magic numbers and strings MUST be replaced with named constants
Feature flags MUST be used for in-progress features rather than commented-out code
Public APIs MUST have documentation describing purpose and usage

practices.title: Use context managers for resource handling

cwe.name: Missing Release of Resource after Effective Lifetime

practices.good:
with open('data.txt') as f:
    data = f.read()
practices.explanation: Manual open/close leaks resources on exceptions. Context managers (with statement) guarantee cleanup.

standards.title: Resource handles (files, connections) MUST be closed in finally blocks or using RAII patterns
Database transactions MUST be rolled back on error
Persistent operations MUST be idempotent or use transaction guards
Resources MUST be released in finally/defer blocks

practices.title: Never use bare except — catch specific exceptions

cwe.name: Detection of Error Condition Without Action

practices.explanation: Bare except catches KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit, hiding real errors. Always catch specific exception types.

standards.title: Exceptions MUST be caught and handled; empty catch blocks are forbidden
All detected error conditions MUST trigger handling logic

practices.title: Guard against None before attribute access
practices.explanation: Accessing attributes on None raises AttributeError. Guard optional values before use.

standards.title: Null/undefined dereferences MUST be guarded before use
Nullable references MUST be checked before dereferencing

practices.title: Handle async errors explicitly — never fire-and-forget
practices.explanation: Fire-and-forget tasks swallow exceptions silently. Always attach error handlers or await tasks.

standards.title: Async operations MUST handle rejection/error paths explicitly
Exceptional conditions MUST be caught and handled appropriately

practices.title: Use generators for large data iteration
practices.bad:
def get_all_rows():
    return [transform(r) for r in db.fetch_all()]
practices.explanation: List comprehensions materialise the entire result in memory. Generators stream one item at a time.

standards.title: Unbounded result sets MUST be paginated or limited
Database connections MUST use connection pooling
Large collections MUST use lazy iteration (generators, streams) instead of full materialization
Caches MUST have eviction policies; unbounded caches are forbidden
Resource consumption MUST be bounded with limits and timeouts

practices.title: Avoid N+1 queries — batch database access

cwe.name: Invokable Control Element with Excessive File or Data Access Operations

practices.bad:
for user in users:
    orders = db.query(f"SELECT * FROM orders WHERE user_id = {user.id}")
practices.good:
user_ids = [u.id for u in users]
orders = db.query("SELECT * FROM orders WHERE user_id = ANY(%s)", [user_ids])
practices.explanation: Querying inside a loop creates N+1 round-trips. Batch into a single query with IN or ANY clause.

standards.title: Database queries inside loops are forbidden (N+1 problem)
String concatenation in loops MUST use a builder or join pattern
Hot-path code MUST NOT perform redundant computation; cache or memoize results
Data access operations MUST be batched; no queries inside loops

practices.title: Avoid mutable default arguments and global mutable state

cwe.name: Data Access Operations Outside of Expected Data Manager Component

practices.explanation: Mutable default arguments are shared across calls, causing subtle bugs. Use None and create fresh instances.

standards.title: Mutable default arguments MUST NOT be used; create fresh instances per call

practices.title: Prefer comprehensions over manual loop-and-append

cwe.name: Invokable Control Element with Excessive File or Data Access Operations

practices.good: result = [x.name for x in items if x.active]
practices.explanation: List comprehensions are faster than manual loop-and-append and communicate intent more clearly.

standards.title: Database queries inside loops are forbidden (N+1 problem)
String concatenation in loops MUST use a builder or join pattern
Hot-path code MUST NOT perform redundant computation; cache or memoize results
Data access operations MUST be batched; no queries inside loops
